title: tropaeolum calvum
intro: Tropaeolum calvum, also known as the 'naked nasturtium', is a species of flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like isothiocyanates and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tropaeolum calvum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs in folk medicine.
